# API & Migrations Compatibility Check configuration

Configuration for the `api-migrations-compatibility.yml` workflow, which diffs the
OpenAPI specs (`api-reference/v1|v2/openapi_spec_*.json`) between the PR and its base
branch using [oasdiff](https://github.com/oasdiff/oasdiff), and lints SQL migrations.

## Files

| File | Used with | Purpose |
|------|-----------|---------|
| `.oasdiff-severity-levels.txt` | `oasdiff breaking --severity-levels` | Overrides the severity (`ERR` / `WARN` / `INFO`) of oasdiff check IDs. Only `ERR` findings fail the job. |
| `.oasdiff-err-ignore.txt` | `oasdiff breaking --err-ignore` | Acknowledged, reviewed breaking-change findings. Each line must contain the HTTP method + path and the finding's description (backticks included) exactly as oasdiff reports it; matching is case-insensitive. |
| `.oasdiff-warn-ignore.yaml` | `oasdiff breaking --warn-ignore` | Warn-level ignores. |
| `migration-rules.yaml` | `validate_migrations.sh` | SQL patterns treated as breaking/warning for migration files. |

> **Format note:** oasdiff parses `--severity-levels` files strictly — one
> `check-id LEVEL` pair per line, **no comments and no blank lines**. Keep all
> commentary in this README.

## OpenAPI 3.0 → 3.1 transition (utoipa 5 upgrade)

The specs are generated by the `openapi` crate. utoipa 5 emits OpenAPI **3.1**,
which encodes the *same* schema differently than the 3.0 output of utoipa 4
(`nullable: true` → `type: [T, "null"]`, nullable `$ref` via `oneOf` instead of
`allOf`, single-value enums as `const`, …). Two measures keep the diff meaningful
across the transition:

1. The workflow up-converts the base spec to 3.1 (`openapi-format --convertTo 3.1`)
   before diffing, so oasdiff compares like-for-like. The step is a no-op once the
   base branch spec is already 3.1.
2. The encoding-level check IDs that still misfire on the utoipa 4 → 5
   representation shift are set to `INFO` in `.oasdiff-severity-levels.txt`
   (the block from `request-parameter-became-not-nullable` to
   `request-parameter-became-enum` at the end of the file). These may be restored
   to `ERR` once the base branch spec is generated by utoipa 5, to maximise
   detection strength.

The entries in `.oasdiff-err-ignore.txt` are findings from the utoipa 5 upgrade PR
that were manually reviewed and accepted: utoipa 5 corrections of previously
mis-documented schemas (e.g. `number_of_installments` was wrongly documented as a
binary string, `feature_data` is genuinely optional) and fields whose documented
shape was fixed to match the actual Rust type. They are scoped to exact
endpoint + property, so they cannot mask unrelated future regressions. Entries may
be pruned once the base branch contains the corrected spec.
